essence: Integrates robots with CNC machines to automate material handling, tool changes, inspection, and end‑to‑end manufacturing workflows.

identity.title: CNC Robotics Engineer
identity.summary: Designs, programs, and maintains robotic systems that work alongside CNC machines, enabling automated loading, unloading, inspection, and high‑efficiency production.

salary.notes: Engineers in aerospace, defence, or fully automated smart‑factory environments can exceed £110k-£150k+.

qualifications: Experience in CNC automation, robotics, or manufacturing engineering
Knowledge of robotic arms, PLCs, sensors, and industrial control systems
Ability to integrate robots with CNC machines and automated workflows
Understanding of safety systems, programming languages, and precision manufacturing

lifestyle.description: CNC robotics engineers work in advanced manufacturing, aerospace, automotive, and smart‑factory environments. They integrate robots with CNC machines, automate material handling, reduce cycle times, and improve precision. The role suits individuals who enjoy robotics, automation, and high‑tech engineering challenges.

habits: Designs and programs robotic systems for CNC integration
Implements automated loading, unloading, and inspection
Optimises cycle times and robotic motion paths
Collaborates with automation, CNC, and manufacturing teams
Ensures safety, reliability, and precision in robotic workflows
